# Figure types {.unnumbered}

This is the catalog. Each chapter takes one kind of figure, shows the data it
expects, and walks from the bare plot to a finished, decorated version you could
drop into a manuscript.

The chapters are ordered roughly by what the figure answers, not by geom. The
survival family comes first -- Kaplan-Meier curves, hazard and nonparametric
curves, number-needed-to-treat -- because that is most of what we publish. Then
the distribution and count plots (histograms, bars, box and density plots),
then the figures that track something over time (spaghetti and trend plots), and
finally the specialty diagrams that answer a specific design question: who
entered the study (CONSORT), how groups flow between states (alluvial and
Sankey), and whether matching worked (covariate balance).

A few chapters -- density, box plots, postage-stamp small multiples -- have no
hvtiPlotR helper. There we use bare `ggplot2` and a house theme, and say so, so
the figure still sits comfortably next to the rest.
